notes_md:
**Ngoan** means *well-behaved, obedient, good (in behavior)*. It’s mostly used for kids, but also for pets or even adults in a cute/joking way.

It describes someone who listens, follows rules, and doesn’t cause trouble. Parents and teachers use it a lot.


Opposite: **hư** (naughty, badly behaved).

content.sentence: Cậu bé thật ___ và lễ phép.

translation.en: The boy is very well-behaved and polite.

example.en: The girl is very well-behaved when doing her homework.
example.vi: Cô bé rất ngoan khi làm bài tập.
example.es: La niña es muy obediente cuando hace su tarea.

translation.en: She is very well-behaved when studying.

exampleSentencesTranslated: His daughter is very well-behaved so everyone loves her.
If you are well-behaved, I will take you to the park.
Parents are always proud because their son is well-behaved at school.
exampleSentences: Con gái của anh rất ngoan nên ai cũng yêu quý.
Nếu em ngoan, chị sẽ dẫn em đi chơi công viên.
Bố mẹ luôn tự hào vì con trai mình rất ngoan ở trường.
notes:
Ngoan means well-behaved, obedient, good (in behavior). It’s mostly used for kids, but also for pets or even adults in a cute/joking way.

It describes someone who listens, follows rules, and doesn’t cause trouble. Parents and teachers use it a lot.

Opposite: hư (naughty, badly behaved).
